topStories.description: President Trump's decision to share the post without further comment has been viewed by observers as a sign of active backchannel diplomacy.

topStories.title: 'Trap Closing Around Trump': Ex-White House Adviser On Iran War
topStories.description: "Donald Trump has not ordered the ships carrying US Marines to reverse course," US national security expert Professor Robert Pepe said

topStories.title: 'Nobody knows what happened to him': Trump on reports regarding Mojtaba Khamenei's health
topStories.description: US President Donald Trump on Monday addressed the rumours regarding the health of Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, saying that nobody knows what happened to him. His remarks came as several reports speculated that the leader is either dead or severely injured.

topStories.title: 'Not Much Time Left To Rescue United States': Iran's Stern Warning To Trump
topStories.description: Iran will "paralyse" and "sink" American vessels if provoked, a senior Iranian official said.

topStories.title: Melania Trump attends her own global summit for just 7 minutes on day one; Israeli PM's wife among attendees
topStories.description: Melania arrived at 10:01 am, addressed attendees, and departed at 10:08 am shortly after completing her speech.

topStories.title: Delta Air Lines scraps priority services for Congress lawmakers citing shutdown strain, long TSA lines
topStories.description: The airline said lawmakers will no longer receive airport escorts, priority rebooking, or complimentary upgrades. However, they will retain access to a dedicated reservations phone line.

topStories.title: What are Iran's demands for truce with US? Explained as Trump confirms talks with Tehran
topStories.description: Signals emerging from within Iran suggest that any move towards negotiations will come with steep and potentially non-negotiable demands.

topStories.title: After call with Trump, PM Modi discusses energy crisis with Sri Lanka President amid US-Iran war
topStories.description: Taking to X, PM Modi stated that his phone call with the Sri Lankan leader focused on the global energy crisis due to the West Asia conflict.

topStories.title: US Supreme Court hears case on whether migrants stopped in Mexico can claim asylum in the US
topStories.description: At the centre of the case is whether migrants stopped on the Mexican side of the border can be considered to have “arrived” in the United States under federal law.

topStories.title: Democrats slam ICE for using excessive force to arrest woman at San Francisco Airport; 'illegal aliens', says DHS
topStories.description: California Democrats criticised ICE after a viral video showed a crying woman being detained at San Francisco airport. DHS said the two women arrested had final removal orders since 2019 and were in the US illegally, adding the action was unrelated to expanded airport enforcement plans.

topStories.title: Modi, Trump discuss West Asia conflict; India backs peace, secure Hormuz Strait
topStories.description: This was the first phone conversation between Modi and Trump since Israel and the US’s military strikes on Iran on Feb 28 triggered a conflict

dailyNews.title: Iran war shows norms of international conflicts have been upended
dailyNews.description: US threats against energy infrastructure, and Iran's retaliation on its Gulf neighbours, signal a clear change.

dailyNews.title: Taliban frees American researcher Dennis Coyle in ‘goodwill’ move after over a year in detention
dailyNews.description: Coyle, a linguist and researcher from Colorado, was arrested in January 2025. Taliban authorities said he violated local laws, without giving details.

dailyNews.title: US Aircraft Carrier That Left Mideast Over Fire Has Other Issues
dailyNews.description: The USS Gerald R. Ford aircraft carrier arrived at a port in Crete on Monday after it had to leave the Middle East — and the war against Iran — when a fire broke out in its laundry area. But the massive ship’s problems run a lot deeper than that.

dailyNews.title: Marco Rubio testifies in former congressman David Rivera's trial over alleged Venezuela lobbying
dailyNews.description: Prosecutors allege Rivera lobbied US officials in 2017 to ease pressure on Nicolas Maduro without disclosing he was paid $20 million by a subsidiary of Venezuela’s state oil company, violating the Foreign Agents Registration Act.

dailyNews.title: India “indispensable” for maintaining a free, open Indo-Pacific: Top US official
dailyNews.description: Colby is the second senior Trump administration official to visit India this month, after US deputy secretary of state Christopher Landau,

dailyNews.title: ‘You can see results’: Rahul terms Modi's foreign policy ‘universal joke’ as Pakistan turns US-Iran mediator
dailyNews.description: Rahul Gandhi also pointedly objected to PM's references to the Covid pandemic when he spoke about the West Asia crisis in his speeches in Parliament this week

dailyNews.title: LaGuardia crash probe focuses on black box data black ATC missteps, runway incursion, and emergency response protocols
dailyNews.description: Authorities from the United States and Canada, including the Federal Aviation Administration, are conducting a joint probe into the incident.

dailyNews.title: Transgender bill passed by voice vote in Lok Sabha
dailyNews.description: Government hails reform, opposition calls it ‘draconian’; Congress MP Jyothimani said the bill has been brought without consultations from transgender people

dailyNews.title: BJP releases candidate list for TTAADC polls, inducts 5 leaders from ally TPC
dailyNews.description: Tripura BJP president Rajib Bhattacharjee said the party has decided to field candidates in all 28 seats of TTAADC and re-nominated seven sitting members

dailyNews.title: North Korean dictator says government will keep cementing nation's 'irreversible status as a nuclear power'
dailyNews.description: North Korean figure Kim Jong Un said the government will bolster the nation's "irreversible status as a nuclear power," The Associated Press reported.

dailyNews.title: Mullin sworn in as DHS chief after GOP fracture forced Dem to save nomination
dailyNews.description: Former MMA champion Markwayne Mullin was sworn in as the ninth U.S. secretary of Homeland Security on Tuesday, replacing outgoing Secretary Kristi Noem.

dailyNews.title: Trump admin says SJSU now faces 'impending enforcement' for transgender volleyball scandal conflict
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump's Department of Education is cracking down on San Jose State University amid an ongoing Title IX conflict over a transgender former volleyball player.

dailyNews.title: House conservatives erupt over Senate GOP, White House deal amid SAVE Act fight
dailyNews.description: Conservative House members are revolting against a Senate GOP deal to end the DHS shutdown, arguing that a budget reconciliation plan for ICE and voter ID is a political trap.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court Seems Open to Trump Request to Block Asylum Seekers at Border
dailyNews.description: A policy of turning back many asylum seekers at the border was rescinded in 2021, but the Justice Department wants the flexibility to reinstate it as a tool for border control.

dailyNews.title: Gregory Bovino’s Final Days: Harsh Words and Few Regrets
dailyNews.description: He was the face of the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown. But as he begins a retirement that was not entirely voluntary, the Border Patrol leader says he did not go far enough.

dailyNews.title: Saudi Leader Is Said to Push Trump to Continue Iran War in Recent Calls
dailyNews.description: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman sees a “historic opportunity” to remake the region, according to people briefed by U.S. officials on the conversations.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Threat to ‘Obliterate’ Iran’s Power Stations Could Constitute a War Crime, Rights Experts Say
dailyNews.description: Intentionally targeting the country’s energy infrastructure could constitute a war crime under international law.

dailyNews.title: Trump and Republican National Committee Lean Toward Dallas for Unusual 2026 GOP Midterm Convention
dailyNews.description: Parties normally hold conventions every four years to nominate presidential candidates, but Republicans hope to hold one this year in the face of midterm headwinds.

dailyNews.title: Thomas Massie Thinks Being Hated by Trump Is ‘Worth It.’ Will Voters Agree?
dailyNews.description: The Republican congressman from Kentucky is a die-hard libertarian who has centered his campaign on his willingness to buck the president. It has bought him the most expensive primary in the country.

dailyNews.title: Russian Oil Shipment Puts Focus on Kremlin Spy Outpost in Cuba
dailyNews.description: Moscow may be challenging President Trump’s effort to choke Cuba’s economy. China also has suspected listening posts on the island.

dailyNews.title: Rubio testifies in criminal trial of ex-congressman accused of illegal lobbying
dailyNews.description: Former Rep. David Rivera of Florida is accused of secretly lobbying for the Venezuelan government during the first Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: Markwayne Mullin sworn in as DHS secretary after Senate confirmation
dailyNews.description: Markwayne Mullin was sworn in as secretary of the Department of Homeland Security on Tuesday, taking the oath of office at the White House one day after winning confirmation in the Senate.

dailyNews.title: Iran war grinds on amid conflicting messages from Trump and Tehran
dailyNews.description: President Trump's optimism about talks to end the Iran war has been tempered by denials from Tehran, and violence that continues to keep the Strait of Hormuz gridlocked.

dailyNews.title: Trump to host King Charles, Dutch royals in separate visits
dailyNews.description: In separate visits, President Trump is planning to host monarchs from the United Kingdom and the Netherlands next month.

dailyNews.title: Pakistan offers to facilitate U.S.-Iran war talks as Trump and Tehran give mixed signals
dailyNews.description: Trump said top U.S. negotiators and their Iranian counterparts have been engaged in "very, very strong talks," but Tehran has denied that claim.
